weather_report:
After midnight, Subiaco lies under a seamless quilt of cloud, the streets muted and gentle, the horizon softened to charcoal and pearl. The air feels cool and even, with no sharp edges; starlight is gone, moonlight diffused, and the UV is essentially asleep. Clarity is low-contrast and kind to the eyes, the sort of night that hushes conversation and makes footsteps sound thoughtful.

The forecast carries this overcast through the small hours into dawn, so expect a gray, even start to the day with sunshine, if it arrives, coming filtered and late. UV will be minimal early and only stir if the ceiling thins toward late morning. It looks quietly pleasant rather than bright: a day for light layers, long sleeves, and a soft jacket; perhaps a scarf if you’re out before sunrise. Consider small, ordinary comforts—brew a proper coffee, sort a to-do list, take an early tram or a measured walk beneath the uniform sky. If you head out later, tuck sunglasses and a touch of sunscreen in your bag on the off-chance the clouds part; otherwise, lean into the calm and get a few tidy errands done while the world stretches awake.
